slides.subtitle: The ten layouts the example pitch doesn't use, rendered with realistic copy so the standalone viewer can be checked against the site.

slides.title: Embedded, not outsourced
slides.body: Our practitioners join your standups, your codebase, and your on-call rotation. The work ships inside your stack, and the knowledge stays when we leave.

slides.title: Production is the only demo that counts
slides.subtitle: Every engagement ends with a system your team runs without us.

items.title: Discovery sprint
items.body: Two weeks mapping the highest-value AI opportunity in your workflow.

items.title: Build team
items.body: Two to four senior practitioners embedded with your engineers.

items.title: Handover plan
items.body: Documentation, training, and a runbook your team owns from day one.

items.label: Week 1
items.title: Scope
items.body: Pick one workflow with measurable value.

items.label: Week 3
items.title: Working model
items.body: A first model running on your data.

items.label: Week 8
items.title: Pilot
items.body: Real users, real load, weekly reviews.

slides.title: How we de-risk delivery
slides.body: Four habits carried into every engagement, whatever the stack.

items.title: Ship weekly
items.body: Working software every Friday, not a report at the end of the quarter.

items.title: Measure impact
items.body: Every model is tied to a business metric before a line of code is written.

items.title: Pair by default
items.body: Your engineers build alongside ours, so capability transfers as we go.

items.title: Exit on purpose
items.body: The engagement is scoped to end — with your team running the system.

slides.title: The delivery pod
slides.body: A small senior team, shaped to the problem rather than to a org chart.

items.title: Lead practitioner
items.body: Owns the outcome and the architecture.

items.title: ML engineers
items.body: Build, evaluate, and productionize the models.

items.title: Your team
items.body: Embedded throughout, running it by the end.

slides.title: Financial services
slides.subtitle: Where model risk is board-level, delivery discipline wins.

items.body: Banks and insurers come to us when a promising pilot meets model-risk review. We build the evaluation harnesses and audit trails that let compliance say yes.
Typical work: document intelligence for underwriting, controls monitoring, and assistant tooling for client-facing teams — all inside the client's perimeter.

slides.title: Teams we've shipped with
slides.subtitle: A sample of logos, loaded from real hosted URLs.

slides.title: Three beliefs about applied AI
bullets: Models are the easy part. The hard part is the workflow around them — data contracts, evaluation, and the human handoffs that decide whether anyone trusts the output.
Small senior teams beat large programs. Every layer between the person building and the person using the system doubles the time to production.
Capability should transfer. If the client can't run the system without us a quarter later, we scoped the engagement wrong.

slides.title: Works on ink, too
bullets: Dark surface flips every foreground ink automatically
The logo mark swaps to its inverted cut
Accent numerals keep their brand color
